title: Introduction to Playwright
description: Test your understanding of Playwright fundamentals and core concepts

questions.question: What is Playwright?
options: A test automation framework for web applications
questions.explanation: Playwright is a modern end-to-end test automation framework developed by Microsoft for testing web applications across different browsers.

questions.question: Playwright supports testing in multiple browsers including Chrome, Firefox, and Safari.
questions.explanation: Playwright supports Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit (Safari) browsers out of the box, enabling cross-browser testing.

questions.question: Which company developed Playwright?
questions.explanation: Playwright was developed by Microsoft and is maintained as an open-source project.

questions.question: Playwright only supports JavaScript for writing tests.
questions.explanation: Playwright supports multiple languages including JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Java, and .NET.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of Playwright over older testing frameworks?
options: It has automatic waiting and retry mechanisms
questions.explanation: Playwright includes built-in auto-waiting and retry mechanisms, making tests more reliable without explicit waits.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a browser engine supported by Playwright?
questions.explanation: Playwright supports Chromium, Firefox (Gecko), and WebKit. Trident (Internet Explorer) is not supported.

questions.question: Playwright can only test web applications, not APIs.
questions.explanation: Playwright can test both web applications and APIs. It includes a powerful API testing feature.

questions.question: What is the primary use case for Playwright?
questions.explanation: Playwright is primarily designed for end-to-end testing of web applications, though it can also be used for API testing.

questions.question: Playwright requires a Selenium server to run tests.
questions.explanation: Playwright does not require Selenium or any external server. It directly controls the browser through native browser protocols.

questions.question: Which feature makes Playwright tests more reliable?
options: Automatic waiting for elements to be ready
questions.explanation: Playwright automatically waits for elements to be actionable before performing actions, eliminating the need for manual waits and reducing flakiness.
